query: Angular structural directive vs attribute directive interview question

competitors.label: Angular Directives Overview
competitors.selectionReason: This is the official directives overview and the strongest canonical baseline for component, attribute, and structural directive categories.
competitors.winReason: The official docs define the directive categories well, but our page goes further on the * microsyntax rewrite and on the senior signal of TemplateRef and ViewContainerRef.
theirStrengths: It cleanly defines the three directive categories and connects them to Angular’s template model.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the * desugaring details and on custom structural-directive implementation follow-ups.

realWorldUseCases: The directive can: • add behavior (listen to events, update host properties) • change styling (classes/styles/attributes) • control the DOM tree by creating/destroying embedded views (structural directives).
followUpConfusion: * is just syntax sugar. Angular rewrites it into an ng-template and binds inputs to the directive on that template.
Structural directives control a template block . They inject TemplateRef (what to render) and ViewContainerRef (where to render).

realWorldUseCases: The docs define directive types precisely, but they are less explicit about how those types map to the practical host-behavior versus DOM-creation distinction in our answer.
actionableExamples: Angular.dev includes enough directive examples that the example comparison remains close.
followUpConfusion: The overview page does not go as far into the microsyntax rewrite or into TemplateRef/ViewContainerRef as our page does.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny desugared ng-template example beside the star syntax so the transformation is visible at a glance.

competitors.label: GreatFrontEnd Angular Basic Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd includes a directly relevant directives section, so it is the right interview-prep comparator for this fundamentals topic.
competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd names the directive categories well, but our page goes deeper on what the star syntax rewrites to and on how custom structural directives actually work.
theirStrengths: It gives a clear and concise directive-type breakdown with simple examples.
theirGaps: It stays lighter on the * desugaring and on the senior-level custom structural-directive mechanics.

realWorldUseCases: The directive can: • add behavior (listen to events, update host properties) • change styling (classes/styles/attributes) • control the DOM tree by creating/destroying embedded views (structural directives).
followUpConfusion: * is just syntax sugar. Angular rewrites it into an ng-template and binds inputs to the directive on that template.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd explains structural versus attribute directives, but it does not push as far into how they map to DOM-creation behavior.
actionableExamples: Its directive examples are concrete enough that the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: The page is lighter on what the star syntax desugars to and on the TemplateRef/ViewContainerRef follow-up.

actionableExamples: Add one small custom attribute directive example beside the structural example to show the contrast more visibly.

competitors.label: Angular University ng-template/ng-container Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This tutorial is a strong competitor because it goes deep on structural-directive desugaring, ng-template, and dynamic template outlets.
competitors.winReason: Angular University stays stronger as a deep structural-directives tutorial, but our page now more clearly answers when TemplateRef/ViewContainerRef matters and when an attribute directive is the safer answer because state must survive.
theirStrengths: It gives a thorough under-the-hood walkthrough of structural directives, ng-template, ng-container, and dynamic template usage.
theirGaps: It is more specialized around structural directives than around the full structural-vs-attribute-vs-component classification.

realWorldUseCases: An attribute directive keeps the same host element alive and just changes host behavior or styling.
followUpConfusion: Bring up TemplateRef/ViewContainerRef only when they ask about * syntax, custom structural directives, or why *ngIf destroys component state while an attribute directive does not.

realWorldUseCases: Angular University also grounds the topic in real template composition scenarios, but our page now makes the preserve-state versus destroy-state contrast much more explicit.
actionableExamples: It still provides more step-by-step examples for ng-template and structural-desugaring patterns than our short trivia page, so the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: It answers the ng-template follow-up well, but our page now better calibrates when to stop at the category level and when to bring up TemplateRef/ViewContainerRef.

actionableExamples: Add one mini custom structural directive snippet that shows createEmbeddedView in context.
